---
title: "Physical Activity List for Recovery Stability"
format: html
---
# Physical Activity List for Recovery Stability

Physical activity supports recovery by lowering stress hormones, improving sleep, lifting mood, and reducing craving intensity for many people. You do not need intense workouts; consistency matters more than intensity.

Movement can also interrupt urge cycles by shifting attention and body state.

Regular movement is associated with improved mood, sleep, and stress tolerance, all of which are protective for sobriety. Planning activity in advance increases adherence during low-motivation days.

## Build Your Activity Menu
| Low-effort options (5-15 min) | Moderate options (20-40 min) | Social options |
|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |

::: {.callout-tip}
### Skill rehearsal

Treat this as a rehearsal for moments when motivation is low and pressure is high.

Schedule three activity sessions this week (at least one from each column).

Repetition builds confidence and makes healthy responses easier to access.
:::
